I do not recognize the legality of your bioluminescent markers across this rift valley.

I must calibrate my ocular receptors to perceive the spectrum of light bleeding through this tear in spacetime.

This spatial anomaly defies the geometric laws of my home system and threatens to destabilize my cellular cohesion.
My atmospheric filters are clogging with these strange, organic spores drifting through the thick air.
These towering carbon-based pillars obstruct my primary sensory array, making long-range scanning nearly impossible.

The atmospheric scrubbers hum a lonely chord against the crushing weight of the silence.

These vibrational waves you emit are disruptive to my primary sensory membranes.
